title: mallotus calcosus
intro: Mallotus calcosus, also known as the Chinese garlic or Chinese wild garlic, is a species of plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae) that is native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Mallotus calcosus has been reported to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce swelling, prevent infection, and promote wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the roots of Mallotus calcosus are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
